title: Why Eating the Rainbow Matters: The Science Behind Colorful Plates
title_zh: 为什么吃“彩虹”很重要：彩色餐盘背后的科学
intro_zh: 本文约1000词，介绍通过不同颜色食物获取多种营养素的小常识，包括红色、橙色/黄色、绿色、蓝紫色食物的具体益处，以及如何搭配出均衡餐盘。

paragraphs.en: Have you ever heard an expert say ‘Eat the rainbow’? This phrase refers to the practice of filling your plate with fruits and vegetables of different colors. Beyond making meals look beautiful, this simple strategy ensures you get a wide range of nutrients your body needs. Each color group offers unique vitamins, minerals, and plant compounds that work together to protect your health. Let's explore what each color brings to your plate.
paragraphs.zh: 你是否听过专家说“吃彩虹”？这个短语指的是让你的餐盘装满不同颜色的水果和蔬菜。除了让饭菜看起来漂亮，这个简单的策略能确保你获得身体所需的各种营养素。每种颜色组都提供独特的维生素、矿物质和植物化合物，它们协同作用保护你的健康。让我们探索每种颜色给你的餐盘带来什么。

paragraphs.en: Red foods, such as tomatoes, watermelon, and red bell peppers, are rich in lycopene. Lycopene is a powerful antioxidant that helps protect cells from damage. Studies show that a diet high in lycopene may lower the risk of heart disease and some cancers. For example, cooked tomatoes actually have more available lycopene than raw ones, so don't hesitate to enjoy tomato sauce. Another red champion is beetroot, which contains nitrates that may help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular function.
paragraphs.zh: 红色食物，如西红柿、西瓜和红甜椒，富含番茄红素。番茄红素是一种强大的抗氧化剂，有助于保护细胞免受损伤。研究表明，高番茄红素的饮食可能降低心脏病和某些癌症的风险。例如，煮熟的西红柿实际上比生西红柿含有更多可利用的番茄红素，所以放心享用番茄酱吧。另一种红色冠军是甜菜根，它含有硝酸盐，可能有助于降低血压并改善心血管功能。

paragraphs.en: Orange and yellow foods get their color from carotenoids, especially beta-carotene, which the body converts into vitamin A. This vitamin is essential for good vision, a strong immune system, and healthy skin. Carrots, sweet potatoes, and pumpkins are excellent sources. A medium carrot provides more than 200% of the daily recommended intake of vitamin A. Additionally, oranges and lemons are packed with vitamin C, a water-soluble vitamin that supports immune function and helps the body absorb iron from plant foods.
paragraphs.zh: 橙色和黄色食物从类胡萝卜素中获得颜色，尤其是β-胡萝卜素，人体将其转化为维生素A。这种维生素对良好视力、强健免疫系统和健康皮肤至关重要。胡萝卜、红薯和南瓜是极好的来源。一个中等大小的胡萝卜提供超过每日推荐摄入量200%的维生素A。此外，橙子和柠檬富含维生素C，这是一种水溶性维生素，支持免疫功能并帮助身体从植物性食物中吸收铁。

paragraphs.en: Green vegetables like spinach, kale, and broccoli are nutritional powerhouses. They are rich in vitamin K, which is important for bone health and blood clotting. They also provide folate, a B vitamin that helps cells grow and function. Dark leafy greens are loaded with lutein, an antioxidant that protects eye health. For instance, one cup of cooked spinach supplies over 1000% of the daily value for vitamin K. Green foods also contain fiber, which aids digestion and helps you feel full longer, making them great for weight management.
paragraphs.zh: 绿色蔬菜如菠菜、羽衣甘蓝和西兰花是营养宝库。它们富含维生素K，对骨骼健康和血液凝固很重要。它们还提供叶酸，一种帮助细胞生长和功能的B族维生素。深色绿叶蔬菜富含叶黄素，一种保护眼睛健康的抗氧化剂。例如，一杯煮熟的菠菜提供超过每日所需维生素K的1000%。绿色食物还含有纤维，有助于消化并让你更长时间感到饱腹，使它们成为体重管理的绝佳选择。

paragraphs.en: Blue and purple foods owe their deep color to anthocyanins, a type of flavonoid with strong antioxidant effects. Blueberries, blackberries, eggplants, and purple cabbage are all excellent choices. Anthocyanins have been linked to improved brain function, reduced inflammation, and a lower risk of heart disease. Some research even suggests that eating blueberries regularly may slow age-related memory decline. Purple vegetables like red cabbage also provide vitamin C and fiber. A 2019 study found that people who ate a variety of colorful fruits and vegetables had significantly lower inflammation markers.
paragraphs.zh: 蓝色和紫色食物的深色归功于花青素，一种具有强抗氧化作用的类黄酮。蓝莓、黑莓、茄子和紫甘蓝都是极好的选择。花青素与改善大脑功能、减少炎症和降低心脏病风险有关。一些研究甚至表明，定期吃蓝莓可能减缓与年龄相关的记忆力下降。紫色蔬菜如紫甘蓝也提供维生素C和纤维。2019年的一项研究发现，吃多种彩色水果和蔬菜的人炎症标志物显著降低。

paragraphs.en: Aiming for a rainbow plate doesn't mean you need to eat every color every day. A simple approach is to include at least three different colors at each meal. For example, a breakfast could be oatmeal (white/brown) with blueberries (blue) and sliced banana (yellow). A lunch salad might combine spinach (green), cherry tomatoes (red), and shredded carrots (orange). Don't forget white and brown foods like onions, garlic, mushrooms, and whole grains — they also offer benefits such as allicin from garlic, which boosts immunity.
paragraphs.zh: 追求彩虹餐盘并不意味着每天都要吃到每种颜色。一个简单的方法是每餐至少包含三种不同的颜色。例如，早餐可以是燕麦片（白色/棕色）配蓝莓（蓝色）和香蕉片（黄色）。午餐沙拉可以混合菠菜（绿色）、樱桃番茄（红色）和胡萝卜丝（橙色）。别忘了白色和棕色食物，如洋葱、大蒜、蘑菇和全谷物——它们也提供益处，比如大蒜中的大蒜素能增强免疫力。

paragraphs.en: In conclusion, eating a variety of colorful foods is one of the simplest and most effective ways to support your overall health. Each color provides a unique set of protective compounds that work together to reduce disease risk. Next time you prepare a meal, challenge yourself to add at least one more color. Your body will thank you. Remember, the key is variety and balance — no single food can provide everything you need, but a colorful plate comes close.
paragraphs.zh: 总之，吃各种色彩丰富的食物是支持整体健康最简单有效的方法之一。每种颜色提供一组独特的保护性化合物，它们协同作用降低疾病风险。下次你准备饭菜时，挑战自己至少多加一种颜色。你的身体会感谢你。记住，关键是多样化和平衡——没有一种食物能提供你所需的一切，但一个色彩缤纷的餐盘可以接近这个目标。

questions.q: What does the phrase 'Eat the rainbow' encourage people to do?
options: Consume a variety of fruits and vegetables of different colors.
Eat only one color group per meal.
questions.explain: 文章第一段明确提到“Eat the rainbow”指的是用不同颜色的水果和蔬菜填满餐盘，以获取多种营养素。选项B正确。选项A错误，作者强调天然食物。选项C错误，文章第六段指出白色和棕色食物也有益处。选项D错误，文章鼓励每餐包含多种颜色。

questions.q: Which of the following foods is mentioned as a good source of lycopene?
questions.explain: 文章第二段指出西红柿、西瓜和红甜椒富含番茄红素。选项C西红柿正确。菠菜（A）是绿色蔬菜，富含维生素K；蓝莓（B）富含花青素；红薯（D）富含β-胡萝卜素。

questions.q: What can be inferred about vitamin A from the passage?
options: It can be directly obtained only from orange foods.
The body can produce it from beta-carotene.
It is not important for vision.
It is found mainly in green vegetables.
questions.explain: 文章第三段指出β-胡萝卜素（存在于橙色和黄色食物中）在人体内转化为维生素A。因此选项B正确。选项A错误，维生素A也可以从其他食物间接获得；选项C错误，维生素A对视力至关重要；选项D错误，绿色蔬菜主要提供维生素K和叶酸。

questions.q: What is the author's main purpose in writing this passage?
options: To compare different colors of vegetables.
To provide scientific evidence for the benefits of colorful foods.
To criticize people who don't eat enough vegetables.
To promote a new diet plan based on single food groups.
questions.explain: 文章从科学角度解释了不同颜色食物所含的营养素及其健康益处，如番茄红素、β-胡萝卜素、花青素等，并引用研究数据支持。主旨是提供科学依据鼓励色彩丰富的饮食。选项B正确。选项A只是手段，不是主要目的；选项C没有批评语气；选项D与文章鼓励多样化的观点相反。
